3903	def _row_routes_to_ledger(
3904	    row: Any, question_terms: "frozenset[str] | set[str]", relevance_floor: float
3905	) -> bool:
3906	    """Per-row body-vs-ledger verdict. POSITIVE judged relevance FIRST, then furniture, then the rest
3907	    (Codex F2 gate iter-1 P1: positive-relevance-first ordering):
3908	      1. A POSITIVE judged ``content_relevance_label`` (relevant / escalated_relevant) => BODY. A real
3909	         upstream judge that SAW the content wins over every heuristic leg (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER), so
3910	         a judged-relevant row whose text incidentally trips a broad furniture regex is NOT demoted.
3911	      2. FURNITURE (``is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable`` OR the F2 placement-only vocab
3912	         ``_contains_f2_placement_furniture``) => ledger. The F2 vocab lives on the PLACEMENT path only,
3913	         never on the shared drop predicate (HC-2); a match is a placement (move to the ledger), never a
3914	         drop — the source stays in the pool + bibliography + disclosure.
3915	      3. A confirmed-OFF judged label (``topic_offtopic_demoted`` True or a demoted/escalated_demoted
3916	         ``content_relevance_label``) => ledger.
3917	      4. A JUDGED numeric ``selection_relevance`` below the floor => ledger (placement, not a drop).
3918	      5. NO judgment => fall back to lexical question-term overlap (``_is_offtopic_for_placement``).
3919	    PURE read of already-computed state; the faithfulness engine is untouched."""
3920	    label = ""
3921	    if isinstance(row, dict):
3922	        label = str(row.get("content_relevance_label", "") or "").strip().lower()
3923	    if label in _POSITIVE_RELEVANCE_LABELS:
3924	        return False  # judged relevant => ALWAYS body (positive-relevance-first; never buried)
3925	    text = _ledger_row_text(row)
3926	    if is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable(text) or _contains_f2_placement_furniture(text):
3927	        return True  # furniture => ledger (placement, never a drop; F2 vocab never feeds a drop seam)
3928	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
3929	        return False
3930	    if row.get("topic_offtopic_demoted") is True or label in _CONFIRMED_OFFTOPIC_LABELS:
3931	        return True
3932	    rel = _row_relevance(row)
3933	    if rel is not None:
3934	        return rel < relevance_floor  # judged below-floor => ledger (placement)
3935	    # Unjudged: lexical off-topic => ledger.
3936	    if _is_offtopic_for_placement(text, question_terms):
3937	        return True
3938	    # I-deepfix-006 E (#1376): an UNJUDGED marketing-only preamble (no finding signal — TRU/AACSB) is
3939	    # DEMOTED to the ledger (kept at weight, never a drop). A real judge (rules 1/4) always wins first.
3940	    if _row_is_marketing_only_preamble(text):
3941	        return True
3942	    return False

3945	def partition_body_and_low_relevance_ledger(
3946	    rows: Any, *, research_question: str = ""
3947	) -> "tuple[list[Any], list[Any]]":
3948	    """Split ordered ``rows`` into ``(body, ledger)`` by JUDGED weight/relevance then FURNITURE/off-
3949	    topic, ordering EACH partition by ``weight * relevance`` DESC. §-1.3 PLACEMENT, NEVER a drop:
3950	
3951	      * ``body``   — rows kept in the report's carry-up prose (on-topic, at/above judged floor).
3952	      * ``ledger`` — rows MOVED BELOW the appendix boundary into the "Low-relevance evidence (kept at
3953	        weight)" section: furniture, confirmed-off-topic, judged-below-floor, or (unjudged) lexically
3954	        off-topic. STILL present in the pool, bibliography, and disclosure — nothing is dropped.
3955	
3956	    CONSERVATION (asserted by the caller's test): ``body`` and ``ledger`` are disjoint and their union
3957	    equals ``rows`` (deduped by identity of order) — no row vanishes, none is duplicated. When the
3958	    kill-switch is OFF, every row stays in ``body`` (byte-identical). PURE; no faithfulness touch.
3959	
3960	    SCOPE (F2 gate iter-3): this row-list form is the general/reusable variant the unit tests exercise.
3961	    The pipeline's raw carry-up surfaces are all ev-id ORDERED (weight order the render relies on), so
3962	    the SHIPPED production entry is the order-preserving ``partition_evidence_base_ids_for_ledger`` —
3963	    which shares the SAME per-row ``_row_routes_to_ledger`` verdict as this function."""
3964	    row_list = list(rows or [])
3965	    if not low_relevance_ledger_enabled() or not row_list:
3966	        return row_list, []
3967	    q_terms = _placement_topic_terms(research_question)
3968	    floor = _ledger_relevance_floor()
3969	    body: list[Any] = []
3970	    ledger: list[Any] = []
3971	    for row in row_list:
3972	        (ledger if _row_routes_to_ledger(row, q_terms, floor) else body).append(row)
3973	
3974	    def _order(partition: list[Any]) -> list[Any]:
3975	        # Stable weight*relevance DESC (a demotion order, never a filter). Index tiebreak preserves the
3976	        # caller's incoming order within an equal-key group so the sort is deterministic.
3977	        indexed = list(enumerate(partition))
3978	        indexed.sort(
3979	            key=lambda pair: (
3980	                -(lambda wr: wr[0] * wr[1])(_ledger_weight_relevance_key(pair[1])),
3981	                pair[0],
3982	            )
3983	        )
3984	        return [row for _, row in indexed]
3985	
3986	    return _order(body), _order(ledger)
3987	
3988	
3989	def partition_evidence_base_ids_for_ledger(
3990	    ev_ids: Any, evidence_pool: Any, *, research_question: str = ""
3991	) -> "tuple[list[str], list[str]]":
3992	    """Order-PRESERVING split of the ordered Evidence-base ``ev_ids`` into ``(body, ledger)`` using the
3993	    same per-row body/ledger verdict as ``partition_body_and_low_relevance_ledger``.
3994	
3995	    The incoming order (already weight*relevance from ``select_unbound_supports_by_weight``) is
3996	    PRESERVED within each partition — the render (``build_evidence_base_section``) relies on that
3997	    order, so this NEVER re-sorts. A ledger id is FURNITURE / confirmed-off-topic / judged-below-floor
3998	    / (unjudged) lexically off-topic — it is MOVED BELOW the appendix boundary into the labelled
3999	    "Low-relevance evidence (kept at weight)" section, and STAYS in the pool + bibliography + disclosure
4000	    (§-1.3 PLACEMENT, never a drop). A pool-absent id stays in ``body`` (the render skips it exactly as
4001	    before — no placement change for a non-resolvable id). Conservation: ``body`` + ``ledger`` equals
4002	    the de-duplicated non-empty input. OFF (``PG_LOW_RELEVANCE_LEDGER=0``) => ``(list(ev_ids), [])``."""
4003	    # HC-7 (Codex F2 iter-1 P2): the OFF check is FIRST, before any dedup / empty-strip, so a
4004	    # PG_LOW_RELEVANCE_LEDGER=0 run feeds the body section the ORIGINAL ids untouched — strictly
4005	    # byte-identical to legacy even when duplicate / empty ids are present in the input.
4006	    #
4007	    # F2 gate iter-6 (Codex NOVEL P0 — decoupled-flag placement-becomes-DROP): the ledger partition is
4008	    # RENDERED only through ``build_evidence_base_section`` — BOTH the Evidence base body AND the ledger
4009	    # section are gated internally by ``PG_BREADTH_EVIDENCE_BASE_SECTION``. If that flag is OFF the ledger
4010	    # section CANNOT render, so routing rows into it while the CWF body-feed (multi_section_generator)
4011	    # consumes only the body partition would make those rows vanish from the report prose AND the
4012	    # bibliography — a §-1.3 DROP. COUPLE the split to the render gate: when the Evidence base surface
4013	    # cannot render, the split returns ``(full-surface, [])`` so every row stays in the body order (fed
4014	    # to CWF), the ledger is empty, and PG_BREADTH_EVIDENCE_BASE_SECTION=0 stays byte-identical to legacy
4015	    # (its documented kill-switch contract). The move-half NEVER fires without the place-half.
4016	    if not low_relevance_ledger_enabled() or not evidence_base_section_enabled():
4017	        return list(ev_ids or []), []
4018	    ids: list[str] = []
4019	    seen: set[str] = set()
4020	    for e in (ev_ids or []):
4021	        e = str(e or "")
4022	        if e and e not in seen:
4023	            seen.add(e)
4024	            ids.append(e)
4025	    if not ids:
4026	        return list(ev_ids or []), []
4027	    pool = evidence_pool or {}
4028	    q_terms = _placement_topic_terms(research_question)
4029	    floor = _ledger_relevance_floor()
4030	    body: list[str] = []
4031	    ledger: list[str] = []
4032	    for eid in ids:
4033	        row = pool.get(eid)
4034	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
4035	            body.append(eid)  # pool-absent => leave in body; the render skips it as before (no change)
4036	            continue
4037	        (ledger if _row_routes_to_ledger(row, q_terms, floor) else body).append(eid)
4038	    return body, ledger
4039	
4040	
4041	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4042	# I-wire-012 (#1326) — FAIL-LOUD chrome-as-claim CANARY on the SHIPPED report.
4043	#
4044	# A render-integrity tripwire: it measures what FRACTION of the report's CLAIM bullets
4045	# are chrome (page furniture that rendered as a finding) and, in ``enforce`` mode above
4046	# a floor, REFUSES to ship — so a chrome regression can NOT ship as
4047	# ``released_with_disclosed_gaps``. It NEVER promotes a unit and NEVER touches a
4048	# faithfulness verdict. Modes (LAW VI, default-safe):
4049	#   off     — explicit opt-out: no telemetry, no enforce.
4050	#   warn    — emit telemetry to the manifest; never fails the run.
4051	#   enforce — DEFAULT (I-wire-013 #1327): emit telemetry AND fail the run (status flip) when rate > floor.
4052	_RENDER_CANARY_MODE_ENV = "PG_RENDER_CHROME_CANARY"
4053	_RENDER_CANARY_FLOOR_ENV = "PG_RENDER_CHROME_CANARY_FLOOR"
4054	_DEFAULT_RENDER_CANARY_FLOOR = 0.05
4055	_RENDER_CANARY_MODES = ("off", "warn", "enforce")
4056	_DEFAULT_RENDER_CANARY_MODE = "enforce"
4057	
4058	# A TOP-LEVEL report bullet (no leading indent) = a claim surface (Key-Findings, the
4059	# per-claim corroboration header, a finding bullet). Indented ``  - SUPPORT:`` /
4060	# ``  - GROUNDED-BUT-WEAK`` sub-bullets (source URLs, not claims) and numbered
4061	# bibliography lines ("1. Title") are deliberately EXCLUDED so the rate measures
4062	# chrome-as-CLAIM, not chrome-as-source-locator.
4063	_TOP_LEVEL_BULLET_RE = re.compile(r"^-\s+(.*\S)\s*$")
4064	_BOLD_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\*\*")
4065	
4066	
4067	def render_chrome_canary_mode() -> str:
4068	    """Canary mode from ``PG_RENDER_CHROME_CANARY`` (off|warn|enforce); default ``enforce``
4069	    (I-wire-013 #1327 — the tripwire enforces by default, not telemetry-only). Fail-loud on an
4070	    UNRECOGNIZED value (LAW II — no silent guessed fallback), mirroring ``render_chrome_canary_floor``.
4071	    ``off`` is the explicit opt-out (telemetry suppressed by the caller; verdict never trips)."""
4072	    raw = os.environ.get(_RENDER_CANARY_MODE_ENV)
4073	    if raw is None or not raw.strip():
4074	        return _DEFAULT_RENDER_CANARY_MODE
4075	    mode = raw.strip().lower()
4076	    if mode not in _RENDER_CANARY_MODES:
4077	        raise ValueError(
4078	            f"{_RENDER_CANARY_MODE_ENV}={raw!r} is not a valid chrome-canary mode "
4079	            f"(expected one of {_RENDER_CANARY_MODES})"
4080	        )
4081	    return mode
4082	
4083	
4084	def render_chrome_canary_floor() -> float:
4085	    """Chrome-as-claim rate floor from ``PG_RENDER_CHROME_CANARY_FLOOR`` (default 0.05).
4086	    Fail-loud on a non-float (LAW II — no silent guessed fallback); clamped to [0, 1]."""
4087	    raw = os.environ.get(_RENDER_CANARY_FLOOR_ENV)
4088	    if raw is None or not raw.strip():
4089	        return _DEFAULT_RENDER_CANARY_FLOOR
4090	    try:
4091	        val = float(raw.strip())
4092	    except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
4093	        raise ValueError(
4094	            f"{_RENDER_CANARY_FLOOR_ENV}={raw!r} is not a float (chrome-as-claim rate floor)"
4095	        ) from exc
4096	    return min(1.0, max(0.0, val))
4097	
4098	
4099	def _report_claim_bullets(report_text: str) -> list[str]:
4100	    """The report's TOP-LEVEL claim bullets, bold markers stripped (PURE)."""
4101	    out: list[str] = []
4102	    for line in (report_text or "").split("\n"):
4103	        m = _TOP_LEVEL_BULLET_RE.match(line)
4104	        if not m:
4105	            continue
4106	        out.append(_BOLD_MARKER_RE.sub("", m.group(1)).strip())
4107	    return out
4108	
4109	
4110	# I-deepfix-001 WS-7 (D3): the chrome canary was BLIND to prose — it scored only top-level claim BULLETS
4111	# (_report_claim_bullets), so an in-prose chrome leak (a leading bare section-header word, an in-text
4112	# "(1, 2)" ref marker, a truncated "(2017)" subject) shipped without tripping the canary (drb_72: 0/33
4113	# bullets flagged while prose leaked). This adds the report's claim-bearing PROSE units to the canary
4114	# DENOMINATOR, screened with the SAME shared predicate, so a prose leak now trips the canary fail-closed.
4115	# MEASUREMENT ONLY — the canary computes a rate/verdict; it NEVER drops or edits a rendered unit (that is
4116	# the render seam's job). Default-ON PG_RENDER_CHROME_CANARY_PROSE; OFF => bullets-only => byte-identical.
4117	_RENDER_CANARY_PROSE_ENV = "PG_RENDER_CHROME_CANARY_PROSE"
4118	_PROSE_UNIT_MIN_CHARS = 40
4119	
4120	
4121	def _render_canary_prose_enabled() -> bool:
4122	    return os.environ.get(_RENDER_CANARY_PROSE_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
4123	
4124	
4125	def _report_prose_units(report_text: str) -> list[str]:
4126	    """The report's claim-bearing PROSE units (substantial non-bullet, non-header paragraph lines),
4127	    EXCLUDING scaffolding sections (Bibliography / Methods / disclosures / Source corroboration / the H1
4128	    question echo) whose legitimate DOIs/URLs are not chrome. PURE. These are the units the chrome canary
4129	    was blind to (it scored only top-level bullets)."""
4130	    out: list[str] = []
4131	    in_scaffold = False
4132	    for line in (report_text or "").split("\n"):
4133	        s = line.strip()
4134	        if not s:
4135	            continue
4136	        hdr = _SECTION_HEADER_RE.match(line)
4137	        if hdr:
4138	            title = hdr.group(2).strip().lower()
4139	            in_scaffold = any(title.startswith(t) for t in _SCAFFOLDING_SECTION_TITLES)
4140	            continue
4141	        if in_scaffold:
4142	            continue
4143	        if _TOP_LEVEL_BULLET_RE.match(line) or _LEADING_BULLET_RE.match(line):
4144	            continue  # bullets are already scored by _report_claim_bullets
4145	        if len(s) >= _PROSE_UNIT_MIN_CHARS:
4146	            out.append(_BOLD_MARKER_RE.sub("", s).strip())
4147	    return out
4148	
4149	
4150	def evaluate_render_chrome_canary(report_text: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
4151	    """Compute the chrome-as-claim rate over the SHIPPED report's claim bullets AND prose units, and the
4152	    canary verdict. PURE (no I/O). ``verdict='fail'`` ONLY in ``enforce`` mode when the rate exceeds the
4153	    floor and there is at least one claim unit — so the caller can flip the run status. In ``warn``/``off``
4154	    the verdict is always ``pass`` (telemetry only). Each chrome unit is screened with the SAME shared
4155	    predicate every composer uses (chrome + truncation), so the canary and the screens can never disagree.
4156	    WS-7 (D3): prose units are added to the denominator (default-ON PG_RENDER_CHROME_CANARY_PROSE) so an
4157	    in-prose chrome leak trips the canary — MEASUREMENT ONLY, no rendered unit is dropped or edited."""
4158	    bullets = _report_claim_bullets(report_text)
4159	    units = list(bullets)
4160	    prose_units = 0
4161	    if _render_canary_prose_enabled():
4162	        prose = _report_prose_units(report_text)
4163	        prose_units = len(prose)
4164	        units = units + prose
4165	    chrome = [b for b in units if is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable(b)]
4166	    total = len(units)
4167	    n_chrome = len(chrome)
4168	    rate = (n_chrome / total) if total else 0.0
4169	    floor = render_chrome_canary_floor()
4170	    mode = render_chrome_canary_mode()
4171	    tripped = bool(total and mode == "enforce" and rate > floor)
4172	    return {
4173	        "mode": mode,
4174	        "floor": floor,
4175	        # WS-7 (D3): denominator now = bullets + prose units. total_claim_bullets kept for back-compat
4176	        # consumers (= total scored units); total_claim_units + prose_units_scored are the explicit names.
4177	        "total_claim_bullets": total,
4178	        "total_claim_units": total,
4179	        "prose_units_scored": prose_units,
4180	        "chrome_claim_bullets": n_chrome,
4181	        "chrome_as_claim_rate": round(rate, 4),
4182	        "verdict": "fail" if tripped else "pass",
4183	        "examples": [b[:120] for b in chrome[:5]],
4184	    }
4185	
4186	
4187	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4188	# I-wire-013 (#1327) iter-3a — RENDER-SEAM CHOKEPOINT.
4189	#
4190	# ONE sanitization pass over EVERY claim-bearing unit of the FINAL assembled report (Abstract,
4191	# Key-Findings bullets, every ``###`` section body INCLUDING multi_section_generator output, the
4192	# Corroborated Weighted Findings citation-split blob, the Conclusion), dropping/repairing chrome +
4193	# truncated units with the now-unblinded predicate. This is the single seam that screens the
4194	# currently-unscreened multi_section_generator output (0 screen calls today). It runs AFTER every
4195	# composer + the header-sanity screen and BEFORE the chrome canary, so the canary reads the cleaned
4196	# artifact and a residual leak still trips it fail-closed.
4197	#
4198	# FAITHFULNESS (FROZEN engine): RENDER hygiene only — it SUPPRESSES a page-furniture / truncated
4199	# unit, NEVER promotes one and NEVER touches a strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / span-grounding verdict.

content.text: Continuing on. Still nothing name-worthy of a flag so far — everything is verbose but professional and precise. Let me read the rest.

content.content:
4200	# Page furniture is not a corroborating source, so suppressing it STRENGTHENS faithfulness.
4201	#
4202	# OVER-STRIP SAFE (the drop-path law "over-strip deletes a real finding, worse than a leak"):
4203	#   * Per-``[N]``-unit granularity — a flagged citation unit is dropped WITH its own trailing marker
4204	#     (marker-paired, so a kept finding never loses its citation and a dropped chrome unit never
4205	#     orphans a marker). The 4005-char Corroborated-Weighted-Findings blob keeps every real finding;
4206	#     only its chrome sub-units drop.
4207	#   * Scaffolding sections (Bibliography / Methods / disclosures / Reliability / Source corroboration
4208	#     / the H1 question echo) are EXCLUDED — their legitimate DOIs/URLs are not chrome.
4209	#   * A unit carrying SUBSTANTIAL real prose plus a glued inline ``#`` header is REPAIRED (cut at the
4210	#     header, prefix kept) — the remainder is dropped only when it independently re-tests as chrome,
4211	#     so a real "Limitations: … most extreme.## Analytical synthesis …" paragraph is never lost.
4212

4291	# I-deepfix-001 H1 (#1344) — SOURCE-INTERNAL inline reference-marker STRIP (REPAIR-first).
4292	#
4293	# THE GAP: a fetched source's OWN in-text citation apparatus ("... productivity rose (1, 2).",
4294	# "... as shown (1; 3; 5).") is lifted verbatim into a verified span and rendered as a claim,
4295	# leaving a bare "(1, 2)" numeral group in the body that is NOT one of the report's OWN ``[N]``
4296	# provenance markers — it points at the SOURCE'S bibliography, not ours (dangling mis-attribution).
4297	# This REPAIRS the sentence in place (removes only the leaked marker; the claim text survives) at
4298	# the single render chokepoint, over every claim unit. High-precision: only a parenthetical whose
4299	# WHOLE content is >=2 short integers separated by comma/semicolon is stripped, so a real
4300	# parenthetical ("(1, 2, or 3 doses were compared)") is never touched (it has non-numeric words) and

4305	_RENDER_SEAM_REF_STRIP_ENV = "PG_RENDER_SEAM_REF_STRIP"

4307	# separated run of >=2 integers (1-3 digits each), optional surrounding spaces. Matches "(1, 2)",
4308	# "(1,2,3)", "(1; 2)", "( 10, 11 )". Does NOT match "(3)" (single), "(2020)" (4-digit year),

4312	_PRE_PUNCT_SPACE_RE = re.compile(r"\s+([.!?,;:])")
4313	_MULTISPACE_RE = re.compile(r"[ \t]{2,}")

4353	def strip_source_internal_refs(text: str) -> str:
4354	    """H1 REPAIR: remove leaked source-internal in-text citation markers (bare "(1, 2)" numeral
4355	    groups) from ``text`` and close the whitespace they leave, keeping the claim prose intact. PURE;
4356	    faithfulness-neutral (the report's OWN ``[N]`` / ``[#ev:...]`` square-bracket markers are never
4357	    matched). Returns ``text`` unchanged when the kill-switch is OFF or no marker is present."""
4358	    if not text or not render_seam_ref_strip_enabled():
4359	        return text
4360	    if not _SOURCE_INTERNAL_REF_RE.search(text):
4361	        return text
4362	    # Preserve the line's ORIGINAL leading indentation (a nested markdown bullet that happens to
4363	    # carry a leaked ref must not be de-indented into a top-level bullet).
4364	    lead = text[: len(text) - len(text.lstrip())]
4365	    out = _SOURCE_INTERNAL_REF_RE.sub("", text)
4366	    out = _PRE_PUNCT_SPACE_RE.sub(r"\1", out)
4367	    out = _MULTISPACE_RE.sub(" ", out)
4368	    return lead + out.strip()

4378	def _split_enrichment_blob_line(
4379	    line: str, known_words: "set[str] | frozenset[str] | None"
4380	) -> "tuple[list[str], int]":
4381	    """H2: split ONE glued enrichment body line (a space-joined run of ``sentence.[N]`` findings)
4382	    into one Markdown bullet per finding, sanitizing each finding through the SAME per-line screen
4383	    (``_sanitize_report_line``) so a chrome/truncated finding still drops. Returns
4384	    ``(bullet_lines, dropped)``. A line that is a header, blank, already a bullet, or splits to a
4385	    single sentence is returned as ``([sanitized_line], dropped)`` (no spurious bulletisation).
4386	    PURE except the lazy sentence-splitter import; faithfulness-neutral."""
4387	    stripped = line.strip()
4388	    # Only split real glued prose. A blank / heading / existing bullet / short single clause is
4389	    # sanitized-in-place, never bulletised (over-split is worse than a leak for a real single line).
4390	    if not stripped or stripped.startswith(("#", "-", "*", "|", ">")):
4391	        clean, dropped = _sanitize_report_line(line, known_words)
4392	        # I-deepfix-001 (drb_72) FIX-D: a bullet whose remainder — after removing the leading list
4393	        # marker AND every citation marker — is empty carries NO claim ("- ", "- [12]"); drop it.
4394	        if empty_bullet_drop_enabled() and clean.lstrip().startswith(("-", "*")):
4395	            _residual = re.sub(r"^[-*]\s*", "", clean.strip())
4396	            _residual = re.sub(r"\[[^\]]+\]", "", _residual)
4397	            if not _residual.strip():
4398	                return ([], dropped)
4399	        return ([clean] if clean.strip() or not dropped else []), dropped
4400	    try:
4401	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
4402	            split_into_sentences,
4403	        )
4404	        sentences = split_into_sentences(stripped)
4405	    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - provenance_generator is stable in-tree
4406	        clean, dropped = _sanitize_report_line(line, known_words)
4407	        return ([clean] if clean.strip() or not dropped else []), dropped
4408	    if len(sentences) <= 1:
4409	        clean, dropped = _sanitize_report_line(line, known_words)
4410	        return ([clean] if clean.strip() or not dropped else []), dropped
4411	    bullets: list[str] = []
4412	    dropped = 0
4413	    for sent in sentences:
4414	        clean, drop = _sanitize_report_line(sent, known_words)
4415	        dropped += drop
4416	        clean = clean.strip()
4417	        if clean:
4418	            bullets.append(f"- {clean}")
4419	    return bullets, dropped
4420

4471	def _sanitize_key_findings_bullet(
4472	    line: str, known_words: "set[str] | frozenset[str] | None"
4473	) -> "tuple[str, int]":
4474	    """FIX 4(c): screen ONE Key-Findings bullet at WHOLE-UNIT granularity. The bullet's prose core
4475	    (list + ``**bold**`` markers stripped) is chrome-tested as a single unit: if chrome, the whole
4476	    bullet is dropped (returns ``("", 1)``); otherwise the bullet is kept BYTE-INTACT. A blank /
4477	    non-bullet line inside the block passes through untouched. Never returns a partially-chopped
4478	    bullet, so the opening ``**`` label can never be severed from its close. PURE."""
4479	    if not line.strip() or not line.lstrip().startswith(("-", "*")):
4480	        return line, 0
4481	    core = _unit_core_for_screen(line)
4482	    if core and is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable(core, known_words=known_words):
4483	        return "", 1  # whole chrome bullet dropped (never a mid-unit chop)
4484	    return line, 0  # real finding kept byte-intact

4487	def _sanitize_corroboration_header(
4488	    line: str, known_words: "set[str] | frozenset[str] | None"
4489	) -> "tuple[str, int]":
4490	    """B6(a): screen ONE corroboration HEADER bullet. A top-level ``- **<claim>** — N verified ...``
4491	    whose CLAIM span is chrome (page furniture promoted to a basket header) has the claim string
4492	    replaced by a neutral placeholder, preserving the verified-source COUNT suffix verbatim. Returns
4493	    ``(clean_line, suppressed)`` where ``suppressed`` is 1 iff a chrome claim was withheld. A
4494	    sub-bullet (indented) or a non-matching line is returned unchanged with 0. PURE; faithfulness-
4495	    neutral (no source / count / verdict ever dropped)."""
4496	    m = _CORROBORATION_HEADER_RE.match(line)
4497	    if not m:
4498	        return line, 0  # sub-bullet / blank / prose — KEEP-ONLY, untouched
4499	    claim = m.group("claim").strip()
4500	    suffix = m.group("suffix") or ""
4501	    if claim and is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable(claim, known_words=known_words):
4502	        return f"- **{_CORROBORATION_CHROME_PLACEHOLDER}**{suffix}", 1
4503	    return line, 0

4506	def _known_word_floor() -> int:
4507	    """The corpus known-word frequency floor (LAW VI). Fail-soft on a non-int / non-positive value
4508	    to the detector default (the allowlist must never be silently emptied)."""
4509	    raw = os.environ.get(_RENDER_SEAM_KNOWN_WORD_FLOOR_ENV, "").strip()
4510	    if not raw:
4511	        return _DEFAULT_KNOWN_WORD_FLOOR
4512	    try:
4513	        return max(1, int(raw))
4514	    except ValueError:
4515	        return _DEFAULT_KNOWN_WORD_FLOOR

4518	def build_known_words_from_evidence(evidence_rows: Any, floor: int | None = None) -> set[str]:
4519	    """The corpus-vocabulary allowlist (the truncation false-positive guard) built from the run's OWN
4520	    fetched source text — every lowercase token occurring >= ``floor`` times across the evidence
4521	    rows' ``direct_quote`` / ``statement`` / ``title`` fields. So a word the corpus genuinely uses
4522	    ("labor", "Acemoglu", "computerisation") never false-flags as a span cut, while an absent
4523	    fragment ("Resea", "hodology") does. Accepts a list of row dicts OR a ``{evidence_id: row}`` map
4524	    (the in-memory ``ev_pool`` shape). PURE; returns an empty set when no source text is available
4525	    (=> the caller's truncation leg is simply skipped, never a wrong drop)."""
4526	    if floor is None:
4527	        floor = _known_word_floor()
4528	    if isinstance(evidence_rows, dict):
4529	        rows: Any = evidence_rows.values()
4530	    else:
4531	        rows = evidence_rows or ()
4532	    freq: Counter[str] = Counter()
4533	    for row in rows:
4534	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
4535	            continue
4536	        for field in _KNOWN_WORD_FIELDS:
4537	            value = row.get(field)
4538	            if isinstance(value, str) and value:
4539	                for word in _BOUNDARY_VOCAB_RE.findall(value):
4540	                    freq[word.lower()] += 1
4541	    return {word for word, count in freq.items() if count >= floor}
4542	
4543	
4544	# An alphabetic vocabulary token (mirrors the detector's _WORD_RE / key_findings._BOUNDARY_WORD_RE).
4545	_BOUNDARY_VOCAB_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'\-]*[A-Za-z]|[A-Za-z]")
4546

4548	def _unit_core_for_screen(text: str) -> str:
4549	    """The screen-test text of a unit: leading ``- `` bullet marker and ``**bold**`` markers
4550	    removed so the predicate judges the prose, not the markdown wrapper. PURE."""
4551	    core = _LEADING_BULLET_RE.sub("", text.strip())
4552	    return _BOLD_MARKER_RE.sub("", core).strip()

4555	def _repair_glued_inline_header(text: str, known_words: "set[str] | frozenset[str] | None") -> "str | None":
4556	    """If ``text`` is substantial real prose with a glued inline ``#`` header welded mid-unit, return
4557	    the kept prefix (the header + remainder dropped) — but ONLY when the remainder independently
4558	    re-tests as chrome, so a real paragraph is never over-stripped. Returns ``None`` when no
4559	    repair applies (the caller then drops the whole unit)."""
4560	    m = _INLINE_HEADER_SPLIT_RE.search(text)
4561	    if not m or m.start() < _MIN_REPAIR_PREFIX_CHARS:
4562	        return None
4563	    prefix = text[:m.start()].rstrip()
4564	    remainder = text[m.start():]
4565	    prefix_core = _unit_core_for_screen(prefix)
4566	    # Keep the prefix only if it is itself clean prose AND the excised remainder is independently
4567	    # chrome (over-strip guard: never drop a remainder that re-tests clean).

4575	def _sanitize_report_line(line: str, known_words: "set[str] | frozenset[str] | None") -> "tuple[str, int]":
4576	    """Sanitize ONE claim-section body line: split it into per-``[N]`` citation units, drop each unit
4577	    (with its own trailing marker) that is chrome or a corpus-grounded truncation cut, and repair a
4578	    unit that is real prose with a glued inline header. Returns ``(clean_line, dropped_count)``. A
4579	    marker-less unit that is the whole short line is dropped iff it is chrome (a standalone ToC /
4580	    masthead line). PURE."""
4581	    parts = _CITATION_SPLIT_RE.split(line)
4582	    segments: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
4583	    i = 0
4584	    while i < len(parts):
4585	        seg_text = parts[i]
4586	        seg_marker = parts[i + 1] if i + 1 < len(parts) else ""
4587	        segments.append((seg_text, seg_marker))
4588	        i += 2
4589	    kept: list[str] = []
4590	    dropped = 0
4591	    # I-wire-017 (#1339) FIX B: when a prose segment is dropped as chrome, the SAME claim's trailing
4592	    # continuation citation markers ("prose[6][7][5]" splits into a prose seg + two marker-only segs)
4593	    # would otherwise survive orphaned. Track the drop and suppress the contiguous marker-only run
4594	    # that immediately follows it. A marker-only run after a KEPT segment stays (it belongs to that
4595	    # kept claim). Withhold-only — the dropped markers' evidence is untouched.
4596	    suppress_trailing_markers = False
4597	    for seg_text, seg_marker in segments:
4598	        if not seg_text.strip() and not seg_marker:
4599	            continue
4600	        # A marker-only continuation segment (no prose, just "[N]") inherits the fate of the prose
4601	        # segment it continues: dropped if that prose segment was dropped, kept otherwise.
4602	        if not seg_text.strip():
4603	            if suppress_trailing_markers:
4604	                dropped += 1
4605	                continue
4606	            kept.append(seg_text + seg_marker)
4607	            continue
4608	        core = _unit_core_for_screen(seg_text)
4609	        if core and is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable(core, known_words=known_words):
4610	            repaired = _repair_glued_inline_header(seg_text, known_words)
4611	            if repaired is not None:
4612	                # Real-prose prefix kept WITH its citation marker (the kept finding stays cited);
4613	                # only the glued-header remainder is excised. The prose survives, so its trailing
4614	                # continuation markers are NOT orphaned — keep them.
4615	                kept.append(repaired + seg_marker)
4616	                dropped += 1
4617	                suppress_trailing_markers = False
4618	                continue
4619	            dropped += 1
4620	            suppress_trailing_markers = True  # orphan this dropped claim's continuation markers
4621	            continue  # whole chrome unit + its marker dropped
4622	        kept.append(seg_text + seg_marker)
4623	        suppress_trailing_markers = False
4624	    # I-deepfix-001 H1 (#1344): REPAIR-first strip of leaked source-internal in-text ref markers
4625	    # ("(1, 2)") on the kept prose. No-op (byte-identical) when the kill-switch is OFF or the line
4626	    # carries no such marker; never touches the report's OWN ``[N]`` / ``[#ev:...]`` square-bracket
4627	    # markers (they are seg_marker parts, and the rule matches PARENTHESES only). Faithfulness-neutral.
4628	    # I-deepfix-006 A/B/C (#1376): also excise fixed boilerplate furniture fragments + raw markup that
4629	    # were welded INSIDE the kept prose. Suppress-only; byte-identical when both kill-switches are OFF.
4630	    cleaned = strip_source_internal_refs("".join(kept))
4631	    cleaned = strip_inline_furniture(cleaned)
4632	    cleaned = strip_inline_markup(cleaned)
4633	    return cleaned, dropped

4639	    return any(low.startswith(s) for s in _SCAFFOLDING_SECTION_TITLES)

4642	def _line_has_claim_prose(line: str) -> bool:
4643	    """True iff ``line`` carries claim-bearing prose (not just blanks or bare ``[N]`` /
4644	    ``[#ev:...]`` citation markers). Strips leading bullet / ``**bold**`` markers and ALL citation
4645	    markers, then checks any real alphabetic content remains. I-wire-017 (#1339) FIX C1 helper: used
4646	    to decide whether a sanitized section body has collapsed to orphaned markers only. PURE."""

4651	def _immediate_parent_is_scaffolding(level: int, header_stack: "list[tuple[int, bool]]") -> bool:
4652	    """True iff the NEAREST strictly-shallower open header (the immediate markdown parent) is a
4653	    scaffolding header — e.g. ``### references`` whose parent is ``## Bibliography``. A non-scaffolding
4654	    header at an intermediate depth BREAKS the chain (it becomes the new parent), so a content section
4655	    under a non-scaffolding parent is NOT protected even if a far ancestor is the report's scaffolding
4656	    ``# Research Report:`` title. ``header_stack`` holds ``(level, is_scaffolding)`` for the open
4657	    ancestor headers, shallowest-first. PURE."""
4658	    for anc_level, anc_scaffolding in reversed(header_stack):
4659	        if anc_level < level:
4660	            return anc_scaffolding
4661	    return False

4664	def _drop_empty_claim_sections(lines: "list[str]") -> "tuple[list[str], int]":
4665	    """I-wire-017 (#1339) FIX C1 post-pass: drop a non-scaffolding ``###``-or-deeper section header
4666	    whose body — after the line-level sanitization already ran — has NO claim-bearing prose left
4667	    (only blank lines / bare orphaned citation markers). Such a section renders as a dangling header
4668	    over "[6][7][5]"; withholding the empty header is suppress-only (the evidence is untouched).
4669	
4670	    A SCAFFOLDING header — by its own title OR by having a scaffolding IMMEDIATE parent
4671	    (``### references`` under ``## Bibliography``) — is always preserved. Any top-level (``#``/``##``)
4672	    header is also preserved (a top-level empty section is left for the operator). Returns
4673	    ``(kept_lines, headers_dropped)``. PURE."""
4674	    kept: list[str] = []
4675	    headers_dropped = 0
4676	    # Open ancestor headers as (level, is_scaffolding), shallowest-first; a header at depth d closes
4677	    # every open ancestor at depth >= d before it is pushed.
4678	    header_stack: list[tuple[int, bool]] = []
4679	    i = 0
4680	    n = len(lines)
4681	    while i < n:
4682	        header = _SECTION_HEADER_RE.match(lines[i])
4683	        if not header:
4684	            kept.append(lines[i])
4685	            i += 1
4686	            continue
4687	        level = len(header.group(1))
4688	        title = header.group(2)
4689	        parent_scaffolding = _immediate_parent_is_scaffolding(level, header_stack)
4690	        header_stack = [hs for hs in header_stack if hs[0] < level]
4691	        own_scaffolding = _is_scaffolding_section_title(title)
4692	        header_stack.append((level, own_scaffolding))
4693	        if (
4694	            level < _MIN_DROPPABLE_EMPTY_HEADER_LEVEL
4695	            or own_scaffolding
4696	            or parent_scaffolding
4697	        ):
4698	            kept.append(lines[i])
4699	            i += 1
4700	            continue
4701	        # Collect this section's body up to (not including) the next header of ANY level.
4702	        body_start = i + 1
4703	        j = body_start
4704	        while j < n and not _SECTION_HEADER_RE.match(lines[j]):
4705	            j += 1
4706	        body = lines[body_start:j]
4707	        if any(_line_has_claim_prose(b) for b in body):
4708	            kept.extend(lines[i:j])  # real content survives -> keep header + body verbatim
4709	        else:
4710	            headers_dropped += 1  # empty content section -> drop header + its marker-only body
4711	        i = j
4712	    return kept, headers_dropped

4744	    # opening ``**`` label from its close (the ``Job Displacement …** …`` mangled bullet defect).
4745	    in_key_findings = False
4746	    kf_whole_unit = key_findings_whole_unit_enabled()
4747	    for line in report_md.split("\n"):
4748	        header = _SECTION_HEADER_RE.match(line)
4749	        if header:
4750	            title = header.group(2)
4751	            in_scaffolding = _is_scaffolding_section_title(title)
4752	            in_corroboration = _is_corroboration_section_title(title)

4757	            # ``render_chrome_screen_enabled()`` so this header path honours the same

4761	                not in_scaffolding
4762	                and render_chrome_screen_enabled()
4763	                and _contains_forensic_chrome(title)
4764	            ):
4765	                removed += 1
4766	                continue
4767	            out_lines.append(line)
4768	            continue
4769	        if in_corroboration and corroboration_screen and line.strip():
4770	            # B6(a): inside the corroboration rollup, screen ONLY the per-claim HEADER bullet's
4771	            # claim string (sub-bullets / blanks pass through untouched via the (line, 0) branch).
4772	            clean_line, suppressed = _sanitize_corroboration_header(line, known_words)
4773	            removed += suppressed
4774	            out_lines.append(clean_line)
4775	            continue
4776	        if in_scaffolding or not line.strip():

4778	            continue
4779	        if in_key_findings and kf_whole_unit:
4780	            # FIX 4(c): WHOLE-UNIT screen — a chrome bullet is dropped whole; a real finding bullet is
4781	            # kept BYTE-INTACT (its verbatim strict_verify-passed sentence + ``**`` label preserved),

4783	            # italic preamble line (no ``-``/``*`` marker) passes through untouched.
4784	            clean_line, dropped = _sanitize_key_findings_bullet(line, known_words)

4793	            bullet_lines, dropped = _split_enrichment_blob_line(line, known_words)

4802	    # I-deepfix-001 (drb_72) FIX-D belt: after the line-level pass, drop any surviving bullet that is

4917	# I-beatboth-011 #7 (#1289) — SAME-WORK CONSOLIDATION identity (§-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-
4918	# DON'T-DROP). Two unbound SUPPORTS members are the SAME WORK when they share a
4919	# normalized DOI, else a folded title PLUS a corroborating discriminator. KEEP ALL
4920	# their URLs as corroborating locators of the ONE work, but COUNT/PRESENT them as ONE
4921	# source (multi-URL corroboration), never N independent sources. Identity ladder
4922	# mirrors the existing ``_m51_canonical_identity`` convention (evidence_selector.py:
4923	# 2781): DOI first, else title(+discriminator), else (fallback) the evidence_id itself
4924	# so a member with neither stays its own distinct unit (never wrongly merged).
4925	#
4926	# I-beatboth-011 #4 (#1289) — P1 OVER-MERGE FIX (§-1.3: NEVER merge distinct works;
4927	# under-merge is safe, over-merge corrupts breadth/attribution). The no-DOI branch
4928	# MUST NOT merge on folded TITLE ALONE — two genuinely DIFFERENT works can share a
4929	# normalized title and would be wrongly collapsed, losing distinct corroborators. So
4930	# the no-DOI key requires the folded title PLUS the FIRST PRESENT corroborating
4931	# discriminator the records share, in this fixed priority order: publication YEAR →
4932	# first-author SURNAME → VENUE/journal → URL HOST. A priority-ordered composite (NOT
4933	# pairwise OR / union-find, which is non-transitive and over-merges through chains) is
4934	# a plain equality key, biased to UNDER-merge. Title-with-no-discriminator stays its
4935	# own ev-id singleton (distinct). This key computation is the SHARED canonical contract
4936	# with ``synthesis/finding_dedup.py`` and is duplicated BYTE-FOR-BYTE there (the
4937	# no-new-source-file rule forbids a shared module).
4938	_DOI_PREFIX_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*(?:doi\s*:?\s*|https?://(?:dx\.)?doi\.org/)", re.IGNORECASE)
4939	_TITLE_NORMALIZE_RE = re.compile(r"[^a-z0-9]+")
4940	_WHITESPACE_RUN_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
4941	# Publication-year validity bounds (SHARED with the selector's _row_year convention
4942	# at evidence_selector.py:769-770 and finding_dedup._row_year). Outside => absent.
4943	_MIN_YEAR = 1900
4944	_MAX_YEAR = 2100

4947	def _normalize_doi(raw: Any) -> str:
4948	    """Normalized DOI for same-work grouping ('' when absent/unusable).
4949	
4950	    SHARED contract with ``finding_dedup._normalize_doi``: strip a leading
4951	    ``doi:`` / ``https://doi.org/`` / ``http://dx.doi.org/`` prefix, lowercase,
4952	    ``rstrip("/")``; a USABLE DOI starts with the ``10.`` registrant prefix.
4953	    """
4954	    doi = str(raw or "").strip()
4955	    if not doi:
4956	        return ""
4957	    doi = _DOI_PREFIX_RE.sub("", doi).strip().lower().rstrip("/")
4958	    # A usable DOI starts with the ``10.`` registrant prefix; anything else is noise.
4959	    return doi if doi.startswith("10.") else ""

4962	def _normalize_title(raw: Any) -> str:
4963	    """Normalized title key for same-work grouping ('' when too short to be safe).
4964	
4965	    SHARED with ``finding_dedup._fold_title``: lowercase, collapse non-alphanumeric
4966	    runs to a single space, strip; require >= 12 chars (a tiny/generic title is an
4967	    over-merge risk).
4968	    """
4969	    title = str(raw or "").strip().lower()
4970	    title = _TITLE_NORMALIZE_RE.sub(" ", title).strip()
4971	    # Guard against an over-merge on a tiny/generic title: require real substance.
4972	    return title if len(title) >= 12 else ""

4975	def _record_title(ev: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
4976	    """The record's title across the schema aliases (``source_title`` is canonical;
4977	    ``title`` / ``page_title`` / ``name`` are the validator-mapped variants).
4978	
4979	    SHARED precedence with ``finding_dedup._row_title`` so the two consolidators
4980	    fold the SAME title field.
4981	    """
4982	    for key in ("source_title", "title", "page_title", "name"):
4983	        value = ev.get(key)
4984	        if value:
4985	            return str(value)
4986	    return ""

4989	def _record_year(ev: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
4990	    """Publication year as a discriminator token ('' when absent/invalid).
4991	
4992	    Reads ``ev['year']`` else ``ev['metadata']['year']`` and validates [1900, 2100]
4993	    — the SHARED convention with the selector's ``_row_year`` (evidence_selector.py:
4994	    793-809) and ``finding_dedup._row_year``.
4995	    """
4996	    val = ev.get("year")
4997	    if val is None:
4998	        meta = ev.get("metadata")
4999	        if isinstance(meta, dict):
5000	            val = meta.get("year")
5001	    if val is None:
5002	        return ""
5003	    try:
5004	        year = int(val)
5005	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
5006	        return ""
5007	    return str(year) if _MIN_YEAR <= year <= _MAX_YEAR else ""
5008

5010	def _first_author_surname(ev: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
5011	    """First-author surname (folded) as a discriminator token ('' when absent).
5012	
5013	    Records carry ``authors`` (a list, family-name-first, e.g. ``["Autor D", ...]``)
5014	    or a singular ``author`` string. The surname is the FIRST whitespace token of the
5015	    first author, lowercased + non-alphanumerics stripped. SHARED with
5016	    ``finding_dedup._first_author_surname``.
5017	    """
5018	    raw = ev.get("authors")
5019	    first = ""
5020	    if isinstance(raw, (list, tuple)):
5021	        for entry in raw:
5022	            if entry and str(entry).strip():
5023	                first = str(entry).strip()
5024	                break
5025	    elif raw:
5026	        first = str(raw).strip()
5027	    if not first:
5028	        single = ev.get("author")
5029	        if single and str(single).strip():
5030	            first = str(single).strip()
5031	    if not first:
5032	        return ""
5033	    surname = first.split()[0] if first.split() else ""
5034	    surname = _TITLE_NORMALIZE_RE.sub("", surname.lower())
5035	    return surname
5036

5038	def _record_venue(ev: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
5039	    """Venue/journal (folded) as a discriminator token ('' when absent).
5040	
5041	    Reads ``venue`` else ``journal``, lowercased with non-alphanumeric runs
5042	    collapsed to a single space and trimmed. SHARED with ``finding_dedup._row_venue``.
5043	    """
5044	    raw = ev.get("venue") or ev.get("journal") or ""
5045	    text = str(raw).strip().lower()
5046	    if not text:
5047	        return ""
5048	    text = _TITLE_NORMALIZE_RE.sub(" ", text)
5049	    return _WHITESPACE_RUN_RE.sub(" ", text).strip()

5052	def _record_host(ev: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
5053	    """URL host (no leading ``www.``) as the WEAKEST discriminator token.
5054	
5055	    Same-work fetches usually span DIFFERENT hosts, so host merges almost nothing —
5056	    it is last in the priority order purely as a safety net. SHARED with
5057	    ``finding_dedup._row_host`` (which delegates to ``_host_of``); the host reduction
5058	    here is inlined (no urllib import already present) but produces the SAME token.
5059	    """
5060	    from urllib.parse import urlparse  # noqa: PLC0415
5061	
5062	    url = str(ev.get("source_url", "") or ev.get("url", "") or "")
5063	    if not url:
5064	        return ""
5065	    host = (urlparse(url).hostname or "").lower().strip()
5066	    if host.startswith("www."):
5067	        host = host[4:]
5068	    return host
5069	
5070	
5071	def _title_discriminator(ev: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
5072	    """The STRICT corroborating discriminator for the no-DOI title branch.
5073	
5074	    I-beatboth-011 #4 P2 hardening (#1289): the no-DOI key MUST be strong enough that
5075	    two DISTINCT works sharing a title cannot merge on a single weak signal. A single
5076	    weak signal alone (year-only or host-only) is NOT enough. The token requires the
5077	    folded title PLUS either:
5078	      * a STRONG discriminator (first-author surname and/or venue) — every present
5079	        STRONG/year signal is folded in (year → author → venue, fixed order), so a
5080	        differing year OR differing author OR differing venue yields a DIFFERENT token
5081	        and the two works do NOT merge; OR
5082	      * two INDEPENDENT WEAK signals (year AND host) when no strong signal is present.
5083	
5084	    HOST IS ENABLING-ONLY, NEVER BLOCKING. Same-work members are the same work fetched
5085	    at DIFFERENT URLs, so they (almost) always differ on host (the ``_record_host``
5086	    safety-net premise + §-1.3). Host therefore appears ONLY as the SECOND weak signal
5087	    alongside year, and NEVER in the strong-path token — otherwise every legitimate
5088	    same-work merge (which spans different hosts) would be blocked.
5089	
5090	    Returns '' when neither a strong signal nor (year AND host) is present, so the record
5091	    stays a title-only singleton and is never merged on title alone. SHARED contract with
5092	    ``finding_dedup._title_discriminator`` (byte-identical key string).
5093	    """
5094	    year = _record_year(ev)
5095	    surname = _first_author_surname(ev)
5096	    venue = _record_venue(ev)
5097	    host = _record_host(ev)
5098	    if surname or venue:
5099	        parts: list[str] = []
5100	        if year:
5101	            parts.append("y:" + year)
5102	        if surname:
5103	            parts.append("a:" + surname)
5104	        if venue:
5105	            parts.append("v:" + venue)
5106	        return "|".join(parts)
5107	    if year and host:
5108	        return "y:" + year + "|h:" + host
5109	    return ""
5110	
5111	
5112	def _samework_url_leg_on() -> bool:
5113	    """Render-side mirror of ``finding_dedup._samework_url_leg_enabled`` — reads the SAME
5114	    ``PG_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG`` env flag (default ON) so the two same-work consolidators are
5115	    always in lockstep. OFF => the url leg is skipped and ``_work_identity`` is byte-identical
5116	    to the prior DOI/title-only keying."""
5117	    import os  # noqa: PLC0415
5118	    return os.getenv("PG_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG", "1").strip().lower() not in (
5119	        "0", "false", "no", "off", "",
5120	    )
5121	
5122	
5123	def _samework_url_norm(ev: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
5124	    """Byte-identical mirror of ``finding_dedup._normalize_source_url`` (GH I-deepfix-003
5125	    #1374, Fable gate P1-C). scheme + lowercased host (leading ``www.`` stripped) + path
5126	    (trailing ``/`` stripped); FRAGMENT dropped, QUERY KEPT (dropping it would over-merge
5127	    identity-in-query works). Requires a NON-EMPTY path (a host-only URL is too coarse => "").
5128	    A blank / unparseable URL returns "" so ``_work_identity`` falls through to DOI/title."""
5129	    from urllib.parse import urlparse  # noqa: PLC0415
5130	    raw = str(ev.get("source_url", "") or ev.get("url", "") or "").strip()
5131	    if not raw:
5132	        return ""
5133	    parsed = urlparse(raw)
5134	    host = (parsed.hostname or "").lower().strip()
5135	    if host.startswith("www."):
5136	        host = host[4:]
5137	    path = (parsed.path or "").rstrip("/")
5138	    if not path:
5139	        return ""
5140	    scheme = (parsed.scheme or "").lower()
5141	    prefix = (scheme + "://" + host) if host else ""
5142	    query = parsed.query
5143	    return prefix + path + (("?" + query) if query else "")
5144	
5145	
5146	def _work_identity(eid: str, ev: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
5147	    """Same-work group key for one member: DOI, else title(+discriminator), else its
5148	    own ev_id.
5149	
5150	    I-beatboth-011 #4 (#1289): the no-DOI branch NEVER merges on folded title ALONE
5151	    (two different works can share a title). It requires the folded title AND the
5152	    FIRST present discriminator (year → first-author surname → venue → host).
5153	    Title-with-no-discriminator falls through to the evidence_id (never a constant),
5154	    so a member with neither a DOI nor (usable title + discriminator) stays its OWN
5155	    distinct unit — consolidation can only MERGE genuine same-work duplicates, never
5156	    collapse unrelated works. Matches ``finding_dedup._same_work_key``.
5157	    """
5158	    # STEP 4 render-parity (GH I-deepfix-003 #1374, Fable gate P1-C): FIRST url leg mirrors
5159	    # finding_dedup._normalize_source_url behind the SAME PG_SAMEWORK_URL_LEG switch, so a
5160	    # no-DOI / weak-title multi-chunk PDF collapses to ONE Evidence-base / CWF entry (not 18).
5161	    if _samework_url_leg_on():
5162	        _u = _samework_url_norm(ev)
5163	        if _u:
5164	            return f"url:{_u}"
5165	    doi = _normalize_doi(ev.get("doi"))
5166	    if doi:
5167	        return f"doi:{doi}"
5168	    title = _normalize_title(_record_title(ev))
5169	    if title:
5170	        discriminator = _title_discriminator(ev)
5171	        if discriminator:
5172	            return f"title:{title}|{discriminator}"
5173	    return f"ev:{eid}"
5174

5176	def _member_quote(ev: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
5177	    """The member's render-safe direct quote (control bytes stripped)."""
5178	    raw = (ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or "")
5179	    return _strip_control_bytes(str(raw)).strip()

5182	def build_verified_span_draft(
5183	    ev_ids: Any, evidence_pool: Any, *, research_question: str = ""
5184	) -> str:
5185	    """Deterministic verbatim-span draft for the enrichment section (FIX K).
5186	
5187	    Emits, in the caller's relevance/weight order, up to ``spans_per_source()`` of each
5188	    WORK's own verbatim sentence-units, each with its legacy ``[ev_id]`` marker placed
5189	    INSIDE the sentence, so ``_rewrite_draft_with_spans`` binds each unit to its best
5190	    in-quote span and ``strict_verify`` validates it.
5191	
5192	    I-beatboth-011 #4 (#1289) — RENDER SAFETY: every emitted span is control-byte
5193	    sanitized (NO NUL / C0 bytes ever reach the report) and a unit exceeding the render
5194	    char bound (a 75K raw-extraction blob) is dropped. A CAPTCHA / Cloudflare security
5195	    stub member is dropped by the junk screen.
5196	
5197	    I-beatboth-011 #7 (#1289) — SAME-WORK CONSOLIDATION (§-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-DON'T-DROP):
5198	    members that are the SAME WORK (same normalized DOI, else title) are grouped into
5199	    ONE multi-citation unit-set — counted/presented as ONE source — instead of N. ALL
5200	    the work's URLs are KEPT as corroborating locators: a corroborator's ``[ev_id]``
5201	    marker is attached to a unit ONLY when that corroborator's OWN quote actually
5202	    contains the emitted unit, so every emitted marker is span-grounded by construction
5203	    (this NEVER changes which members verify — the representative would verify alone;
5204	    corroborators only ADD already-grounding co-citations). A truncated-intro duplicate
5205	    of the representative contributes its URL as a corroborator, not a separate source.
5206	
5207	    A source whose quote is boilerplate/chrome/CAPTCHA, pool-absent, or has no
5208	    substantive unit contributes nothing. Returns the joined draft ("" => caller renders
5209	    the existing gap stub, never a silent success). NO LLM, NO faithfulness-gate touch.
5210	    """
5211	    pool = evidence_pool or {}
5212	    is_junk = _make_junk_screen()
5213	    budget = spans_per_source()
5214	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344 SPAN-TOPICALITY): precision-safe per-span off-topic WITHHOLD
5215	    # inputs (empty question OR flag OFF => empty term set => no span ever withheld =>
5216	    # byte-identical). §-1.3 WITHHOLD-and-disclose per span; the source stays in the pool.
5217	    span_question_terms = (
5218	        _question_topic_terms(research_question)
5219	        if offtopic_span_suppress_enabled()
5220	        else frozenset()
5221	    )
5222	    span_min_content_words = _offtopic_span_min_content_words()
5223	    span_min_local_terms = _offtopic_span_min_local_terms()
5224	    # Corpus-wide on-topic vocabulary, computed ONCE (Codex-P1 iter-2 answer): the per-span
5225	    # off-topic gate KEEPS any span sharing vocabulary with the whole corpus, so lower-level /
5226	    # synonym / acronym on-topic spans are retained; only spans foreign to the ENTIRE corpus
5227	    # are withheld. Empty when the gate is disabled (byte-identical no-op).
5228	    span_corpus_terms = _corpus_topic_terms(pool) if span_question_terms else set()
5229	
5230	    # Group ev_ids into same-work buckets, PRESERVING the caller's relevance/weight
5231	    # order: a work's bucket is keyed by its FIRST-seen member, so the highest-ordered
5232	    # member of each work is the representative and the bucket order is the work order.
5233	    work_order: list[str] = []
5234	    work_members: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
5235	    for ev_id in (ev_ids or []):
5236	        eid = str(ev_id or "")
5237	        if not eid:
5238	            continue
5239	        ev = pool.get(eid)
5240	        if not isinstance(ev, dict):
5241	            continue
5242	        direct_quote = _member_quote(ev)
5243	        # Drop CAPTCHA stubs / boilerplate at the WHOLE-member level up front.
5244	        if not direct_quote or is_junk(direct_quote):
5245	            continue
5246	        key = _work_identity(eid, ev)
5247	        if key not in work_members:
5248	            work_members[key] = []
5249	            work_order.append(key)
5250	        work_members[key].append(eid)
5251	
5252	    parts: list[str] = []
5253	    for key in work_order:
5254	        member_eids = work_members[key]
5255	        representative = member_eids[0]
5256	        rep_ev = pool.get(representative)
5257	        if not isinstance(rep_ev, dict):
5258	            continue
5259	        rep_quote = _member_quote(rep_ev)
5260	        units = _substantive_units(rep_quote, is_junk=is_junk)
5261	        # I-deepfix-001 (#1344 SPAN-TOPICALITY): WITHHOLD confidently-foreign spans of an
5262	        # otherwise-on-topic source from citation (fail-open; source never dropped).
5263	        units = _withhold_offtopic_spans(
5264	            units, rep_ev, span_question_terms,
5265	            span_min_content_words, span_min_local_terms,
5266	            corpus_topic_terms=span_corpus_terms,
5267	        )
5268	        if not units:
5269	            continue
5270	        # Pre-compute each corroborator's sanitized quote ONCE for the contains-check.
5271	        corroborator_quotes = [
5272	            (m, _member_quote(pool[m]))
5273	            for m in member_eids[1:]
5274	            if isinstance(pool.get(m), dict)
5275	        ]
5276	        for unit in units[:budget]:
5277	            # The representative ALWAYS grounds (its own quote contains the unit). Attach
5278	            # a corroborator marker ONLY when that corroborator's own quote also contains
5279	            # the unit — so every emitted marker is span-grounded and no member's verify
5280	            # status changes. ALL same-work members retain their URL as a co-locator;
5281	            # those whose fetch differs simply do not add a (would-fail) marker.
5282	            markers = [representative]
5283	            for m_eid, m_quote in corroborator_quotes:
5284	                if unit in m_quote and m_eid not in markers:
5285	                    markers.append(m_eid)
5286	            parts.append(_emit_unit(unit, markers))
5287	    return " ".join(parts)
5288	
5289	
5290	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5291	# I-deepfix-001 WS-3 (#1344) — NUMBERED "Evidence base" breadth surface.
5292	#
5293	# THE LEAK (drb_72 "12 of 88 cited"): the FULL ordered unbound-SUPPORTS surface from
5294	# ``select_unbound_supports_by_weight`` is already UNCAPPED (weighted_enrichment.py:295,469,573),
5295	# but nothing RENDERS it as a numbered reference block, so most span-verified sources never
5296	# receive a ``[N]`` citation. This surfaces EVERY source that carries a surviving isolated-
5297	# ``SUPPORTS`` span into ONE numbered "Evidence base" section: one numbered entry per distinct
5298	# WORK (same-work CONSOLIDATION, §-1.3 — never N entries for one work), each entry = that work's
5299	# OWN verbatim span unit(s) tagged with its ``[ev_id]`` marker(s) so the downstream bibliography
5300	# numberer assigns each a real ``[N]``.
5301	#
5302	# §-1.3 — WEIGHT-and-CONSOLIDATE, never FILTER-and-CAP: this is SURFACING breadth (keep-all), NOT a
5303	# cap / floor / thinner. The input ``ev_ids`` is already the uncapped ordered surface; this ONLY
5304	# renders it. Faithfulness is UNTOUCHED — each emitted unit is a verbatim span carrying a legacy
5305	# ``[ev_id]`` marker that flows through the UNCHANGED ``_rewrite_draft_with_spans`` -> ``strict_verify``
5306	# exactly like every other section; a unit that cannot ground is dropped by that gate, never padded.
5307	# The SAME render-safety screens as ``build_verified_span_draft`` apply (control-byte strip, junk
5308	# screen, per-unit char bound, same-work consolidation). Default-ON;
5309	# ``PG_BREADTH_EVIDENCE_BASE_SECTION=0`` => returns "" => no section => byte-identical legacy output.
5310	_ENV_EVIDENCE_BASE_SECTION = "PG_BREADTH_EVIDENCE_BASE_SECTION"
5311	_EVIDENCE_BASE_TITLE = "Evidence base"
5312	
5313	
5314	def evidence_base_section_enabled() -> bool:
5315	    """Kill-switch ``PG_BREADTH_EVIDENCE_BASE_SECTION`` (default ON). OFF => the numbered
5316	    Evidence base section is not rendered (``build_evidence_base_section`` returns "") =>
5317	    byte-identical legacy output."""
5318	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_EVIDENCE_BASE_SECTION, "1").strip().lower() not in (
5319	        "0", "false", "no", "off",
5320	    )
5321	
5322	
5323	def build_evidence_base_section(
5324	    ev_ids: Any,
5325	    evidence_pool: Any,
5326	    *,
5327	    start_index: int = 1,
5328	    research_question: str = "",
5329	) -> str:
5330	    """Render the FULL ordered unbound-SUPPORTS surface as ONE numbered "Evidence base" markdown
5331	    section so EVERY source with a surviving span gets a ``[N]`` citation.
5332	
5333	    ``ev_ids`` is the ordered surface from ``select_unbound_supports_by_weight`` (already uncapped —
5334	    NO cap / floor / thinner is applied here). Members are CONSOLIDATED into same-work buckets
5335	    (§-1.3 keep-all-count-once): one numbered entry per distinct work, in the caller's
5336	    relevance/weight order, each carrying its verbatim span unit(s) + the ``[ev_id]`` marker(s) the
5337	    downstream span-binder + ``strict_verify`` re-check.
5338	
5339	    Returns "" when the flag is OFF, ``ev_ids`` is empty, or no surfaced source yields a substantive
5340	    verbatim unit (caller renders nothing — byte-identical). NO LLM; NO faithfulness-gate touch.
5341	    """
5342	    if not evidence_base_section_enabled():
5343	        return ""
5344	    ev_ids = list(ev_ids or [])
5345	    if not ev_ids:
5346	        return ""
5347	    pool = evidence_pool or {}
5348	    is_junk = _make_junk_screen()
5349	    budget = spans_per_source()
5350	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344 SPAN-TOPICALITY): precision-safe per-span off-topic WITHHOLD
5351	    # inputs (empty question OR flag OFF => empty term set => no span ever withheld).
5352	    span_question_terms = (
5353	        _question_topic_terms(research_question)
5354	        if offtopic_span_suppress_enabled()
5355	        else frozenset()
5356	    )
5357	    span_min_content_words = _offtopic_span_min_content_words()
5358	    span_min_local_terms = _offtopic_span_min_local_terms()
5359	    # Corpus-wide on-topic vocabulary, computed ONCE (Codex-P1 iter-2 answer): the per-span
5360	    # off-topic gate KEEPS any span sharing vocabulary with the whole corpus, so lower-level /
5361	    # synonym / acronym on-topic spans are retained; only spans foreign to the ENTIRE corpus
5362	    # are withheld. Empty when the gate is disabled (byte-identical no-op).
5363	    span_corpus_terms = _corpus_topic_terms(pool) if span_question_terms else set()
5364	
5365	    # Same-work grouping, PRESERVING the caller's relevance/weight order (mirrors
5366	    # ``build_verified_span_draft``: a work's bucket is keyed by its FIRST-seen member so the
5367	    # highest-ordered member of each work is the representative and bucket order is work order).
5368	    work_order: list[str] = []
5369	    work_members: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
5370	    for ev_id in ev_ids:
5371	        eid = str(ev_id or "")
5372	        if not eid:
5373	            continue
5374	        ev = pool.get(eid)
5375	        if not isinstance(ev, dict):
5376	            continue
5377	        direct_quote = _member_quote(ev)
5378	        if not direct_quote or is_junk(direct_quote):
5379	            continue
5380	        key = _work_identity(eid, ev)
5381	        if key not in work_members:
5382	            work_members[key] = []
5383	            work_order.append(key)
5384	        work_members[key].append(eid)
5385	
5386	    lines: list[str] = []
5387	    number = start_index
5388	    for key in work_order:
5389	        member_eids = work_members[key]
5390	        representative = member_eids[0]
5391	        rep_ev = pool.get(representative)
5392	        if not isinstance(rep_ev, dict):
5393	            continue
5394	        rep_quote = _member_quote(rep_ev)
5395	        units = _substantive_units(rep_quote, is_junk=is_junk)
5396	        # I-deepfix-001 (#1344 SPAN-TOPICALITY): WITHHOLD confidently-foreign spans of an
5397	        # otherwise-on-topic source from the numbered breadth surface (fail-open).
5398	        units = _withhold_offtopic_spans(
5399	            units, rep_ev, span_question_terms,
5400	            span_min_content_words, span_min_local_terms,
5401	            corpus_topic_terms=span_corpus_terms,
5402	        )
5403	        if not units:
5404	            continue
5405	        # Pre-compute each corroborator's sanitized quote ONCE for the contains-check (same-work URLs
5406	        # co-cite ONLY when their own fetch actually contains the emitted unit — span-grounded markers).
5407	        corroborator_quotes = [
5408	            (m, _member_quote(pool[m]))
5409	            for m in member_eids[1:]
5410	            if isinstance(pool.get(m), dict)
5411	        ]
5412	        emitted: list[str] = []
5413	        for unit in units[:budget]:
5414	            markers = [representative]
5415	            for m_eid, m_quote in corroborator_quotes:
5416	                if unit in m_quote and m_eid not in markers:
5417	                    markers.append(m_eid)
5418	            emitted.append(_emit_unit(unit, markers))
5419	        if not emitted:
5420	            continue
5421	        lines.append(f"{number}. {' '.join(emitted)}")
5422	        number += 1
5423	
5424	    if not lines:
5425	        return ""
